rationale: In the citing text, Mishra and Mishra (2011) is not just listed in a broad citation cluster; it is invoked directly to support a key theoretical premise that lower prices can serve as purchase justification. That premise is used in hypothesis development (predicting lower-class preference for same/lower-priced options) and again in the contribution section as a contrast point. The new paper positions its main contribution as a qualification of that prior pricing-justification pattern rather than a method transfer or mere background mention.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Mishra and Mishra (2011) as prior evidence that lower prices or discounts can justify purchases in vice-like contexts. It then qualifies that logic by showing a boundary condition where higher prices can also justify socially costly purchases for upper-class consumers via entitlement.
